T2.DMM 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Getting Started with the Concept Management Model Package

    Transform DAP-specific concept map into a normalized long format

    wrangling_concept_map.Rd

    This function reshapes a DAP-specific concept map (formatted according to the RWE BRIDGE metadata specification) into a normalized long format suitable for downstream processing and application of codelists. It expands the mapping by melting multiple `column_name_*` and `expected_value_*` columns, and assigns an `id_set` identifier to each original mapping entry.

    wrangling_concept_map(dap_specific_concept_map)

    Arguments

    dap_specific_concept_map

    A `data.table` formatted according to the **RWE BRIDGE metadata scheme**, including at least the following columns:

    concept_id

    The harmonized concept identifier.

    cdm_table_name

    Name of the CDM table where the mapping applies.

    keep_value_column_name

    Column name whose value should be kept when applying the mapping.

    keep_date_column_name

    Column name containing the date associated with the record.

    column_name_*

    One or more columns specifying CDM columns to map.

    expected_value_*

    One or more columns specifying the expected code values corresponding to the CDM columns.

    Value

    A `data.table` in long format with the following columns:

    id_set

    Unique identifier for each mapping set.

    concept_id

    Harmonized concept identifier.

    cdm_table_name

    CDM table name (original).

    cdm_column

    Name of the CDM column being mapped.

    code

    Expected code for the CDM column.

    keep_value_column_name

    Column to retain as the value.

    keep_date_column_name

    Column to retain as the date.

    order_index

    Order index for hierarchical application of mappings.

    Details

    This function:

    1. Adds a unique identifier column `id_set` for tracking each mapping set.

    2. Dynamically identifies all `column_name_*` and `expected_value_*` columns.

    3. Converts the mapping into a long format, where each row corresponds to a specific combination of CDM column and expected value.

    4. Assigns an `order_index` based on the original column order.

    The resulting structure supports hierarchical processing of concept mappings when applying them to a CDM database.

    Examples

    if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Assuming dap_specific_concept_map follows the RWE BRIDGE format:
long_map <- wrangling_concept_map(dap_specific_concept_map)
} # }

    Contents
